# charts

<!-- archie:ai-start -->

> Organisational parent for the two Helm charts (benthos-collector, openmeter) that package all OpenMeter Kubernetes workloads. Each child chart is independently versioned and published to GHCR OCI registry; this folder owns the shared helm-docs generation pipeline (Makefile + template.md) used by both charts.

## Patterns

**helm-docs via shared template** — README for each child chart is generated by `helm-docs` using the shared template.md defined here. Run `make docs` from this folder to regenerate both READMEs. Never hand-edit a chart README. (`make docs  # invokes helm-docs with template.md for all child charts`)
**OCI Helm registry distribution** — Charts are packaged with `helm package deploy/charts/<CHART> --version <VERSION> --destination build/helm` and pushed to ghcr.io/openmeterio/helm-charts/. The version and appVersion fields in Chart.yaml must stay aligned with the container image tags. (`helm package deploy/charts/openmeter --version 1.2.3 --destination build/helm`)

## Key Files

| File | Role | Watch For |
|------|------|-----------|
| `deploy/charts/Makefile` | Runs helm-docs across both child charts using the shared template.md. The only command is `make docs`. | Ensure helm-docs is available in the environment (it is in the Nix CI shell). Running without it silently produces no output. |
| `deploy/charts/template.md` | Shared Go-template for helm-docs that defines badge helpers (versionBadge, typeBadge, appVersionBadge, artifactHubBadge) and the tldr / chart.base layout consumed by both child README templates. | Editing badge or section templates here affects both child chart READMEs. ArtifactHub badge URL is derived from `.Name`, so chart names must not change without updating ArtifactHub listings. |

## Anti-Patterns

- Hand-editing a child chart README.md — it is generated by helm-docs from template.md and values.yaml comments
- Adding a third chart here without a corresponding helm-docs README.tmpl.md and updating the Makefile
- Storing shared Kubernetes manifests (CRDs, cluster-scoped RBAC) at this level — each chart must be self-contained
- Publishing charts without aligning Chart.yaml appVersion with the container image tag used in values.yaml

## Decisions

- **Shared template.md at deploy/charts level rather than duplicated per chart** — Both charts share the same badge set and TL;DR install pattern; centralising the template ensures consistent branding and reduces duplication across helm-docs runs.
- **OCI registry (ghcr.io) as the distribution channel, not a traditional Helm repository index** — OCI registries are supported natively by Helm 3+ and integrate with the same GHCR credentials used for container images, simplifying the release pipeline.

<!-- archie:ai-end -->
